name: google-play-store
description: Google Play Store regime rubric — encodes Google Play Developer Program Policies (restricted content, deceptive behavior, malware, user data, families/children), the Data safety form requirements, and the annual targetSdkVersion / compileSdkVersion enforcement deadlines. Opt-in via project-config.yaml `compliance: ["google-play-store"]`. Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition (base + regimes + domain + project).

metadata.title: Google Play Store regime rubric
metadata.name: google-play-store
metadata.description: Google Play Store policy, data-safety form, and target SDK regime rubric.

severity_rules.pattern: App ships content covered by Google Play's Restricted Content policy without the corresponding content rating or eligibility marker — gambling features outside permitted geographies, sexual content, hate speech, real-money gambling, regulated goods (alcohol, tobacco, prescription drugs), or graphic violence.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's Restricted Content policy bars or geographically restricts categories such as sexual content, hate speech, real-money gambling, regulated goods, and graphic violence. Shipping such content without the matching content rating or geographic gating is a top-tier policy violation that triggers app removal.
severity_rules.remediation: Remove the disallowed content, or — when narrowly permitted — apply the appropriate content rating in Play Console, configure country-of-availability restrictions, and document compliance evidence for review.

severity_rules.pattern: App impersonates another developer or app, makes misleading functional claims (fake antivirus, fake performance booster), spoofs system UI (fake system dialogs / notifications), or manipulates metadata (keyword stuffing, ratings manipulation) to mislead users.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's Deceptive Behavior policy prohibits impersonation, misleading functionality claims, system-UI spoofing, and metadata manipulation. Violations are a top rejection cause and grounds for developer-account termination.
severity_rules.remediation: Remove impersonating branding, align store listing with actual functionality, replace system-UI mimics with non-deceptive UI, and audit listing keywords/screenshots for accuracy.

severity_rules.pattern: App contains or downloads at runtime code matching the Malware policy — trojan/backdoor behavior, on-device cryptocurrency mining, privilege escalation, runtime DEX/JAR loading from untrusted sources, or exploitation of accessibility services for unrelated purposes.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's Malware policy bans trojans, on-device crypto mining (without a documented exception), privilege escalation, runtime code execution from untrusted sources, and abuse of accessibility services. Violations cause immediate removal and account-level enforcement.
severity_rules.remediation: Remove the offending code path, ship all executable code in the APK/AAB itself, restrict accessibility-service usage to its declared purpose, and prohibit on-device crypto mining unless Play has granted a documented exception.

severity_rules.pattern: App declares or uses dangerous / runtime / special permissions (READ_CONTACTS, ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION, RECORD_AUDIO, CAMERA, READ_SMS, QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES, MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, accessibility services) without an in-app prominent disclosure or a justified declared use that matches Play Console's permissions declaration form.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's User Data policy mandates that any dangerous, runtime, or special permission be tied to a clearly disclosed in-app feature, requested via the Android runtime permission flow, and declared on the Play Console permissions declaration form when applicable. Undeclared or over-broad permission use is one of the top rejection drivers.
severity_rules.remediation: Audit AndroidManifest.xml against actual feature use, drop unused permissions, add prominent in-app disclosure before requesting sensitive permissions, and complete the matching Play Console permissions declaration (location background, SMS/Call Log, accessibility, package visibility) where required.

severity_rules.pattern: App targets children or a mixed audience (target audience set to under-13 in Play Console, or app is enrolled in Designed for Families) but ships SDKs / ad networks not on the families self-certified ads SDK list, collects PII without verifiable parental consent, or omits the required teacher-approved content criteria.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's Families policy and the Designed for Families program impose stricter ad-SDK allowlists, COPPA-aligned consent requirements, and content rating gates when an app's audience includes children. Misalignment is a top family-policy rejection cause.
severity_rules.remediation: Replace non-self-certified ad SDKs with families-program approved versions, gate any PII collection behind verifiable parental consent, and verify content rating + teacher-approved criteria against the Designed for Families requirements before opt-in.

severity_rules.pattern: Code collects user data types listed in the Data safety taxonomy (location, personal info, financial info, health/fitness, contacts, app activity, web browsing history, app info/performance, device IDs) without a matching Play Console Data safety form `data_collected` declaration.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's Data safety form requires the developer to enumerate every data type the app or its SDKs collect. Missing or under-declared data types are a Data safety form policy violation and a frequent enforcement target.
severity_rules.remediation: Map every data-collecting code path and SDK against the Data safety taxonomy, declare each collected data type in Play Console, and re-audit on every dependency upgrade.

severity_rules.pattern: Project bundles third-party SDKs that transmit user data off-device (advertising, analytics, attribution, A/B-testing, crash-reporting) without declaring `data_shared` entries in the Play Console Data safety form covering each SDK's transmitted data types.
severity_rules.description: Sharing user data with third parties — including advertising and analytics partners — must be declared in the Data safety form's `data_shared` section. Undeclared sharing breaches the form and is a recurring enforcement signal.
severity_rules.remediation: Inventory all SDKs that transmit data off-device (Firebase, AdMob, AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Mixpanel, Sentry, etc.), declare the corresponding `data_shared` entries in Play Console, and refresh after every SDK upgrade.

severity_rules.pattern: Data safety form declarations omit or misclassify the purpose category — app functionality, analytics, developer communications, advertising, fraud prevention, security, compliance, personalization, account management — for one or more declared data types.
severity_rules.description: Each declared data type in the Data safety form must be assigned at least one purpose category that accurately reflects its handling. Mis-mapped or omitted purposes invalidate the declaration and are flagged in Play Console reviews.
severity_rules.remediation: Re-classify each declared data type with the correct purpose categories — keep purposes minimal and aligned with code paths; document rationale in `_memory/` or compliance artifacts.

severity_rules.pattern: Project declares data transmission off-device but does not enforce TLS / HTTPS in the network security config (cleartextTrafficPermitted true, missing network_security_config.xml, HttpsURLConnection bypass) or omits a user-initiated data-deletion mechanism when the Data safety form claims one.
severity_rules.description: The Data safety form's security-practices section attests to `data_encrypted_in_transit` and the existence of a `data_deletion_mechanism`. Cleartext traffic or a missing in-app/web deletion path falsifies those attestations and breaches the form.
severity_rules.remediation: Enable network_security_config.xml with cleartextTrafficPermitted=false, route all off-device traffic through TLS, and ship a documented user-initiated data-deletion flow (in-app or via a web URL) that matches the Play Console attestation.

severity_rules.pattern: App collects or shares any user data but the Play Console listing or AndroidManifest does not surface a publicly accessible privacy policy URL covering collection, use, sharing, retention, deletion, and contact information.
severity_rules.description: Google Play's Data safety form and User Data policy require a privacy policy URL that is publicly accessible, applies to the app, and covers the data lifecycle. A missing or 404 policy URL is a hard rejection trigger.
severity_rules.remediation: Publish a privacy policy at a stable HTTPS URL covering collection, use, sharing, retention, deletion, and contact info; reference the URL in Play Console and in any in-app About / Settings screen.

severity_rules.pattern: Module's `targetSdkVersion` (build.gradle / build.gradle.kts / app-level Android Gradle Plugin DSL) is below the configured `config.min_target_sdk` threshold (default API 34, Android 14).
severity_rules.description: Google Play enforces an annually-rising minimum targetSdkVersion. As of the August 2024 deadline new and updated apps must target at least API level 34 (Android 14); API 35 (Android 15) is the expected 2025 floor. Apps below the threshold are blocked from new submissions and updates.
severity_rules.remediation: Bump targetSdkVersion to the configured threshold (default 34), test against scoped storage, foreground-service types, and the latest runtime-permission changes; coordinate the bump with the annual Play deadline.

severity_rules.pattern: Module declares `compileSdkVersion` (or `compileSdk`) lower than its `targetSdkVersion`, breaking Android Gradle Plugin's invariant and producing builds that may not exercise newer platform APIs the manifest claims to target.
severity_rules.description: Android Gradle Plugin requires `compileSdkVersion` >= `targetSdkVersion`. A mismatch yields builds that target a new API level without compiling against it, leading to unguarded API calls and runtime crashes — and is flagged by the Play Console pre-launch report.
severity_rules.remediation: Set `compileSdkVersion` (`compileSdk` in newer DSL) to at least `targetSdkVersion` — usually the latest stable API level — and rebuild against the newer SDK.

severity_rules.pattern: Project's `targetSdkVersion` will be below the next Google Play annual API-level deadline (configured via `config.annual_deadline_date`) — typically the August deadline rolling forward each year (API 34 in 2024, API 35 in 2025) — by the deadline date.
severity_rules.description: Google Play enforces an annual rolling targetSdkVersion deadline (commonly August 31). Apps that fail to bump in time lose the ability to ship updates and, ultimately, are removed from Play. This rule fires when the configured deadline approaches and the project still targets a sub-threshold API level.
severity_rules.remediation: Plan the annual targetSdkVersion bump well before the configured `config.annual_deadline_date`; track Play's published deadline schedule and update both the rubric config and the app's build.gradle ahead of time.
